stem: A retail study measures customer satisfaction score across 4 chains, each containing multiple stores. chain A has 8 observations with mean 61.4 /10 (within-chain SD = 3.3). chain B has 10 observations with mean 63.9 /10 (within-chain SD = 6.2). chain C has 15 observations with mean 53.9 /10 (within-chain SD = 4.4). chain D has 5 observations with mean 50.7 /10 (within-chain SD = 5.7). The between-chain standard deviation is uncertain, estimated between 8.6 and 21.6 /10. Using a hierarchical Bayesian model, estimate the true customer satisfaction score for chain B, accounting for shrinkage toward the grand mean and the uncertainty in the between-chain variation.

ground_truth.answer: Hierarchical estimate for chain B: 63.8 /10 (shrunk from raw 63.9). Range [63.6, 63.8] as between-chain SD varies from 8.6 to 21.6. Pooled mean: 57.7.

solution_steps: Compute pooled (grand) mean: 57.7 /10 (weighted average of 4 chain means).
Shrinkage factor for chain B: B = σ²_between / (σ²_between + σ²_within/10). At σ_between=14.4: B = 0.982.
Interval [63.6, 63.8] captures uncertainty from the unknown between-group variance.
common_failure_modes: Using the raw observed mean (63.9) without shrinkage
Using only the grand mean (57.7) and ignoring group-specific data
Not recognizing that small-sample groups should be shrunk more toward the grand mean
Treating the between-group variance as known rather than uncertain
